ab: karuwa ku kiumaumalr kaninu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: karuwa, wawai ku.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: karuwa ku kiumumalr kaninu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: ’azi sazu ku kinazalram zi amanan kiumalray ku.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: miwari u nu alusu a kadaw? maranger ku mukuwa kiumaumalra kaninu kararuwa u nay?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: ai, kuwa ngarayaw u i ruma’.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: miwari u nu ’emanan? maranger ku zuwa kiumaumalra kaninu kana vativatiyan na Pinuyumayan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於卑南族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary story of the Pinuyumayan.

ab: auwa ku i zenan nu ’emanan zi, ’azi kuy kaazu i ruma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: mikiningeran ku za salaw u mazalram kana tu kakuwayanan na kinawatranan na Pinuyumayan zi, maranger ku mukuwa kipanadama kaninu.
ch: A:我聽說您對卑南族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Pinuyumayan, so I would like to ask you something.

ab: ’azi sazu ku kinazalram, maw mu nanku kinazalram ziya mu kuy tatuvangaw u.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya kararuwa ku pasirep semasing awa? nu ta kuwaniyaw mu nu dadaman lra mara madulu lra nu venavalray ta.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: ai, kuwatreng za eman.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya pidaya lra nu ’ami harem?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: makaiwa mikasaleman za waluwaluwa lra ku ’ami.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: tatena’ lralrak u ziya.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: ’azi, dawdawan ku lra.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya kemay i iyan u?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: kemay i Katratripulr ku.
en: B: I come from Chimoto.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya kaninu i turuma’anu mu mawu a Pinuyumayan?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是卑南族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Pinuyumayan?

ab: melri, intaw mu maw na Amis.
en: B: No, she’s an Amis.

ab: sayhu u marengay kana emaeman na piningaiyan?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: sayhu ku marengay kana ngai za Pinuyumayan zi ngai za Amis.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I can speak Amis and Pinuyumayan.

ab: kana wari harem kasa ku semangal karuwa ku kiumaumalr kaninu, alra rengay ziya, kana pinakapalisi na watralunan mu?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ai. a lralrak ku ziya mu marayas ku muzenan paalrupa.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: lrayuwan, salaw ’inava nanu ipanadam kaninku harem kana wari.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: naku irarengay mu maw na tatena’ peniya, tu iyavilin marengay nanta temuwamuwan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: harem kana wari na ku kiyaumaumalr ta palruwanay kania. salaw lrayuwan kilrawa’ kananu kinazalraman.
ch: A:今天我的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: This is the end of my interview today. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: amanan na maira, masalra ta lra mazayazayar.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: averuk ku lra, mengaraya ta ziya nu masalr mu maruwaya ku ziya kinger za marasazu za vativatiyan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: ai! alra tawar lra.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
